CPI - Intraday post (13/Jan)

Weekly post:

January OpEx week quant breakdown | Weekly post (12-16/Jan) - FREE

Last weekly post upside target was hit.

The weekly post is entirely free. It contains the key levels, and expected dynamics and my derivation of sentiment from the vol markets.

CPI’was made’ very cautious today. Core CPI came out a touch cooler (2.6% vs 2.7%), unchanged vs November (2.6%). Not a huge narrative revolution. It’s more like “good enough” to reinforce disinflation hopes, while not screaming acceleration neither recession.PPI is coming tomorrow…

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

Very clear profile today. Net speed profile is negative, downside vol is heavily shorted, market shows no fear, however today theupside vol is also confidently sold, hinting me that market expects stabilization, while no bet on short vvol today. Move is expected.

Centroid is at 6955.06 - 6967.78During the PM it functions as a line in the sand too.

Upside pivot is at 7004.25Target is 7022.05, that becomes sticky (margin of error is 7040)However the pivot is actually a pivot. Market wants lower RV there, otherwise 6985 will pin.

Downside pivot is at 6944.18Target is 6929.83Also sticky, with margin of error of 6900

Given the CPI print, the main bet is ranging, liquidity hunting. Secondary bet is an upward stabilization. Third is downward stabilization at the downside target.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
